summary.intent: Fix new code review comments on PR #480 in the moltis repo, specifically around the ACTION_KEYS constant and rescue pattern, then resolve all review conversations.
summary.outcome: Added 'patch' and 'job' to ACTION_KEYS in cron_tool.rs, committed and pushed, replied to and resolved all review threads on the PR.

repo: cron_tool.rs uses a const ACTION_KEYS to exclude action-level keys when rescuing flat params into a synthetic object in the None branch
The rescue pattern was refactored into a shared helper rescue_stringified_object(&mut Value) used by 5 call-sites
PR is in the moltis-org/moltis repo, PR #480

code.finding: ACTION_KEYS constant excludes action-level keys (including 'patch' and 'job') from being copied into synthetic objects during flat-param rescue in the None branch

workflow: GitHub GraphQL API required to resolve review threads (REST API not sufficient for resolving conversations)
PR review flow: edit code -> run tests -> format -> commit -> push -> reply to review comments via API -> resolve threads via GraphQL

friction: Resolving review threads requires GraphQL queries to get thread IDs first, then separate mutation calls per thread — no simple bulk-resolve endpoint
Multiple round-trips needed: fetch comments, get full bodies, get thread IDs, reply, resolve — each a separate API call
open_items: Verify whether 'job' exclusion from ACTION_KEYS is truly necessary in the None branch or if it was added for consistency/future-proofing
